data.title: S&P, Nasdaq hit new highs on strong jobs report ahead of Independence Day holiday weekend
data.description:
Wall Street scaled a new all-time high on Thursday, as market participants snapped up equities and dumped bonds after nonfarm payrolls jumped more than expected in June.

Trading volume is expected to be low, as the U.S. stock market will close early on account of the July 4 Independence Day holiday.  

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.8% in late morning trade, touching another new high and eyeing the 6,300 points level, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) followed suit, +1%. The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.8%.    

“The stock market is starting off the second half of 2025 on a strong foot, with stocks continuing to make record highs as investors start to price in fading tariff uncertainty and optimism over tax cuts and continued economic resiliency,” said David Laut, chief investment officer at Abound Financial.

“With stocks at record highs, we are sticking with the winners, which includes growth stocks, which have led the market since the April lows. Growth has led us out of the downturn, and we would expect this category to broaden out across company market caps, especially as interest rates decline,” Laut added.

June nonfarm payrolls came in at +147K vs. +110K consensus and +144K in May (revised from +139K), while the unemployment rate was 4.1% vs. 4.3% consensus and +4.2% prior.

The stronger-than-expected jobs report calmed fears of a slowdown in the U.S. economy. It also quelled expectations of an interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve in July.

“Thursday's jobs report was stronger than expected, which shows that the resiliency we have been seeing in the economy over the past several months is still intact. We still expect the Federal Reserve to continue its wait and see approach on interest rates, pushing any potential rate cuts into the fourth quarter of this year,” said Laut.

Over in the bond market, yields jumped as bonds sold-off. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 5 basis points to 4.33%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 9 basis points to 3.89%.

Also on Thursday's economic calendar, initial jobless claims unexpectedly slipped in the previous week, while the U.S. goods and services trade deficit expanded in May. 

U.S. stocks closed at a record high on Wednesday, after U.S. President Donald Trump said a trade deal had been reached with Vietnam.

data.title: JPMorgan revamps private bank to help superrich clients invest globally - report
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) is overhauling its private bank to meet the growing demand from ultra-wealthy clients who want their money invested beyond where they live, according to a media report published on Thursday.

David Frame has been named global chief of the elite private bank, which requires a minimum client balance of $10M, the Wall Street Journal reported, citing Mary Erdoes, JPMorgan's head of asset and wealth management arm, in an interview. Previously, Frame served as the U.S. head.

The revamp comes as growing trade and geopolitical risks have prompted the superrich to seek diversification across borders, wary of tying too much wealth to any one part of the world.

"Our clients have always been multi-jurisdictional but now their assets are too, and that's becoming ever more so with how the world is changing," Erdoes told the WSJ, noting JPMorgan's (NYSE:JPM) white-glove bank has already been working on helping clients invest their funds worldwide.

JPM shares drifted up 1.5% in late morning trading.

data.title: Venture Global scores new 20-year LNG contract with Malaysia's Petronas
data.description:
Venture Global (NYSE:VG) said Thursday it agreed to a new 20-year sales and purchase agreement with Malaysian state-owned energy firm Petronas, joining a list of global customers from Europe and Asia in securing supply of liquefied natural gas from the CP2 development.

According to the deal terms, Petronas will acquire 1M metric tons/year of LNG from Venture Global's (NYSE:VG) CP2 LNG facility in Louisiana, the company's third liquefaction project.

With the new agreement, Venture Global (VG) said it has secured contracts for ~10.75M metric tons/year out of the 14.4M tons/year total capacity planned for Phase 1 of the CP2 project, covering around three-quarters of the initial output.

The latest agreement builds upon Venture Global’s (VG) existing agreement with Petronas for 1M tons/year of LNG supply from Plaquemines LNG, also on the Louisiana coast.

data.title: Elon Musk's xAI issued permits for 15 natural gas turbines at Memphis data center: report
data.description:
• Elon Musk's xAI has been issued permits for 15 natural gas turbines at its Memphis data center, TechCrunch reported.
  • The permits were issued by Shelby County Health Department and allow xAI to operate 15 Solar SMT-130 generators and generate up to 247 MWs in power, the news outlet added.
  • The maker of the Grok chatbot recently raised $10B in funding from investors, including $5B in debt and $5B in equity, Morgan Stanley, the firm's banker, said earlier this week. The capital funding announcement came days after it was reported that xAI was burning through roughly $1B per month.
  • In March, xAI acquired Musk's social network X in an all-stock deal, which propelled xAI to a $113B valuation.
  • Musk, who also leads SpaceX (SPACE) and Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA), said in July 2024 he would bring the idea of Tesla investing $5B into xAI to the electric vehicle maker's board of directors after the idea received nearly two-thirds of “yes” votes in an online poll.
  • The startup is expected to generate $13B in annual earnings by 2029, Bloomberg reported, citing documents from Morgan Stanley.

data.title: Oracle's Stargate deal demonstrates strength of hyperscalers: BNPP
data.description:
Oracle's (NYSE:ORCL) expanded role in the Stargate Project reinforces BNP Paribas Exane's belief that hyperscalers remain of the best ways for investors to take advantage of the rapid rise of artificial intelligence.

OpenAI plans to rent 4.5 gigawatts of data center capacity from Oracle for the Stargate Project. This includes increasing the capacity of its Abilene, Texas, data center to 2 gigawatts from 1.2 gigawatts. 

This provides another example of the importance of hyperscalers in the age of AI. BNP Paribas rates Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Oracle at Outperform due to their roles in building out AI infrastructure. 

Oracle shares were up 1% during Thursday market action after climbing 5% on Wednesday to reach a new, all-time high of $229.98.

BNP Paribas estimates that providing 4.5 GW of data center capacity for the Stargate Project could generate $30B to $60B of annual revenue, depending on the hourly rental price per GPU. However, it remains unclear if Oracle will be responsible for financing all the build out or if Stargate partners will contribute as well. The financial firm estimates that it will require $180B to $225B to fund the 4.5 GW of Stargate capacity.

"We also note that this deal may indicate that Stargate may be near formation, which may indicate that Microsoft and OpenAI may be near completion of their renegotiation," said BNP Paribas Exane analysts in an investor note. "We see this as a significant catalyst for Microsoft as investors may begin to look at OpenAI as an asset rather than a liability (and may begin to embed value for OpenAI into Microsoft's valuation, but also future revenue potential for Azure if Microsoft continues to provide all of the inference for OpenAI, who is targeting $125bn of revenues in 2029)."

data.title: Summit said to be in talks with Astra to license potential rival for Merck’s Keytruda
data.description:
Summit Therapeutics (NASDAQ:SMMT) shares spiked on Thursday, triggering a trading halt after Bloomberg News reported that AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) is in discussions to strike a licensing  deal with the company for its lung cancer drug ivonescimab, a potential rival for Merck’s (NYSE:MRK) Keytruda.

Citing people familiar with the matter, the publication reported that the Miami, Florida-based biotech can earn up to $15B over time from the licensing deal. Summit (NASDAQ:SMMT) has held talks with other major drugmakers, the people said, requesting anonymity as the matter remains private.

While the terms are not finalized yet, Summit (NASDAQ:SMMT) is expected to receive an upfront payment worth several billion dollars in addition to a few milestone payments from the Anglo-Swedish pharma giant, some of the people added.

The companies could still walk away from the discussions, and Summit (SMMT) can seek a different partner, the people said.

Ivonescimab, SMMT’s lead asset developed with its Chinese partner Akeso (OTCPK:AKESF), has already outperformed Merck’s (NYSE:MRK) PD-1 inhibitor Keytruda in a late-stage trial as a first-line treatment for lung cancer.

data.title: Google looks to help publishers amid growing AI search worries - The Information
data.description:
Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) is hiring new staff to market its publisher advertising technology to big advertisers and ad agencies, according to a report by The Information on Thursday, citing two publishing executives.

The move comes after recent media reports suggested that traffic on publishers' websites has slowed due to the company's AI-powered search results.

A spokesperson for the tech giant told The Information that the company had been investing for years in its business to help publishers sell ads and grow.

“Like the rest of the industry, we’re also focused on helping publishers better connect with agencies directly,” the spokesperson reportedly said.

Publishers get "some of the lowest prices on the ad space" sold by Google Ad Manager when compared to rival ad tech firms like PubMatic (PUBM) and Magnite (MGNI), two publishers told The Info.

That's in part because mostly small businesses buy ads on publisher sites through Google’s tools, and they’re less likely to pay a premium for ad space, they said.

In a push to put more resources into publisher tools, Google launched "Offerwall" last week, which allows readers different ways of paying for content, such as paying directly or watching an ad before reading an article.

data.title: Riot sees 76% Y/Y rise in June bitcoin production
data.description:
Riot Platforms (NASDAQ:RIOT) said on Thursday that it produced 450 bitcoins in June, down 12% from the prior month but 76% higher year-over-year.

Average bitcoins produced per day was 15 in June.

The company held 19,273 bitcoins as of June-end, and sold 397 bitcoins for net proceeds of $41.7M the previous month.

Average net price per bitcoin sold came at $105,071. BTC (BTC-USD) was trading at $109,919 during morning trade.

The deployed hash rate for June was 35.5 EH/s, while the average operating hash rate was 29.8 EH/s.

data.title: Carlyle to offload up to 10% stake in India's Piramal Pharma - report
data.description:
Carlyle Group (NASDAQ:CG) is set to sell up to a 10% stake in Indian drugmaker Piramal Pharma, in a deal that could fetch the U.S.-based private equity firm 26B-27B rupees ($304.3M-$316.0M), Moneycontrol reported on Thursday, citing sources.

Carlyle (NASDAQ:CG) bought a 20% stake in Piramal in 2020 for around $490M, the article said.

CG shares gained 2.2% in late morning trading.

data.title: Hargreaves Lansdown’s three stocks for the long term
data.description:
U.S. stocks (SP500), (COMP:IND), (DJI) have rebounded strongly after a challenging start, as investors have now shifted their focus from tariff concerns to potential earlier-than-expected rate cuts and easing tensions in the Middle East, said Derren Nathan, head of equity research at Hargreaves Lansdown.

While the “Magnificent Seven” tech giants (GOOGL), (AMZN), (AAPL), (META), (MSFT), (NVDA), (TSLA) continue to dominate headlines, “there’s plenty of quality on the U.S. exchanges,” he said, and pointed out three stocks for the long-term:

Intuitive Surgical (NASDAQ:ISRG) stands out as a promising long-term investment with its revolutionary robot-assisted surgical platform delivering “huge benefits to patients, surgeons and healthcare providers,” Nathan said.

The company has achieved an average revenue growth of 14.6% over the past decade, with only a single down year in 2020 due to Covid-19’s impact on health systems. Also, while the company trades on a premium multiple, successful product rollouts could potentially drive “significant upside to market forecasts,” he said.

Oracle (NYSE:ORCL) has emerged as “a formidable force in cloud computing” alongside its established leadership in data storage and business software, Nathan added. Its rising prominence was recently underscored by a substantial $30B deal with OpenAI, further enhancing its already substantial revenue backlog of $138B.

Oracle’s (NYSE:ORCL) competitive advantage lies in how “smoothly it connects its powerful data storage with flexible cloud services,” allowing it to compete effectively against tech giants like Amazon (AMZN), Google (GOOGL), and Microsoft (MSFT).

Finally, Caterpillar (NYSE:CAT), with its nearly century-long history of manufacturing “mission-critical heavy machinery,” presents another compelling investment opportunity despite recent challenges from “weak construction activity,” Nathan said.

The company is well-positioned to benefit from “ambitious infrastructure plans in the U.S.” and increasing “demand for minerals critical to the energy transition,” which should boost its mining equipment sales. Furthermore, “the AI boom not only presents an opportunity to benefit from data center construction,” but also drives demand for Caterpillar’s power generation products, Nathan said.

Lastly, while Caterpillar’s (NYSE:CAT) share price “has recovered strongly of late,” potential investors might consider it “one to buy on the dips” given possible “short-term challenges ahead.” This tactical approach reflects the broader market sentiment that despite current volatility, companies with strong fundamentals and alignment with major technological and infrastructure trends remain attractive for long-term investment strategies.
